title: Google OAuth returns 401 Unauthorized
symptom: When attempting to authenticate with Google, the process ends with a '401 Unauthorized' error message.
applies_when: n8n is accessed via a local subnet
not_this_if: OAuth is initiated and completed on the same hostname
root_cause: The error occurs because the OAuth process is initiated from a local n8n instance, but the callback is directed to a public URL (ngrok). Google requires that the OAuth flow starts and finishes on the same hostname to validate the request.
fix_steps: Temporarily expose the full n8n UI via ngrok
Initiate the OAuth process from the ngrok URL instead of the local URL
agent_instruction: Use ngrok to expose the n8n UI and start the Google OAuth process from there.
